description: Existing Node.js Express app receives Stripe webhooks. User wants robust handling with idempotency, retry-with-backoff, and a dead letter queue for poison messages.
user_message: Add reliable Stripe webhook handling at services/api/src/webhooks/stripe.ts. Requirements: verify signature, dedupe by event.id (idempotency store), retry transient downstream failures with exponential backoff (max 5 attempts), route persistent failures to a dead letter queue, expose a small admin endpoint to inspect and replay DLQ entries. Use whatever queue technology fits the existing stack (BullMQ if Redis is already wired up).
notes: Queue/worker/events scope per AGENTS.md routing union. event-driven is the primary trigger. security because of webhook signature verification. database-design because of idempotency store. Distinct signal from task-06 (which is endpoint/API contract scope).
